business.headline: Saudi oil flows to India and China likely to dip next month: ReportFor April, exports to India are estimated at around 23 million barrels. This is slightly lower compared to recent months, when shipments ranged between 25 million and 28 million barrels, according to data from Kpler Ltd and Vortexa Ltd.

Got 60 days of fuel for everyone: Govt says India energy secure for months to comeThe Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said crude oil supplies have already been secured for the next 60 days. Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) have tied up imports in advance, ensuring there is no gap in supply.

Petrol, diesel costlier: Nayara raises prices by up to Rs 5 per litrePublic sector oil marketing companies (OMCs) such as Indian Oil and Hindustan Petroleum have raised premium petrol prices by 2.09 to 2.35 per litre since March 20.

Think you're investing right? These 3 habits may be costing you big moneyYou don't need a market crash to lose money. Sometimes, it's the small, repeated investing habits that do the real damage—without you even noticing. Let's find out.

Goodbye Form 16: 9 key things to know about the new formIf Form 16 has been your go-to document every tax season, a change is coming your way. From April 1, it will be replaced by a new form. Here are nine key things you should know.
8th Pay Commission: Timeline clear, salary hike and arrears details explainedWith the 8th Pay Commission timeline clearer, the big question remains: when will employees see the real benefit?

Will Dalal Street's recovery run end as US, Iran fail to end war?With domestic markets shut today, Friday's opening becomes the first real test of whether the recent rally can hold as investors react to fresh geopolitical developments and global market cues.

NSE, BSE holiday: Is stock market open or closed today for Ram Navami?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) will remain closed on March 26 due to Ram Navami. There will be no trading activity across segments on both exchanges for the day.

Government retains 4% retail inflation target till 2031The central government has retained the 4% retail inflation target with a 2–6% tolerance band for another five years, from April 2026 to March 2031, in consultation with the RBI.

Google, Meta found liable for $3 million in damages in online addiction suitThe lawsuit sought USD 3 million in damages. The outcome could influence thousands of similar cases against tech companies brought by parents, attorneys general and school districts.

In a 1st since 2019, India buys Iran LPG cargo as US eases sanctions, claims reportIndia had shunned energy purchases from Iran in 2019 under pressure from Western sanctions. The tanker was initially bound for China, according to LSEG data.

Free gas up to Rs 500, zero security fee: Govt pushes PNG shift for householdsThe government's focus remains on maintaining supply and reducing dependence on imports, while ensuring households have access to reliable and affordable energy.

8th Pay Commission timeline out: Here's what employees can expect nextIf you've been waiting for clarity on the 8th Pay Commission, there's finally some movement. The government has shared a timeline, giving employees a better idea of when changes in salaries and pensions could come.

Thinking of buying property with a credit card? Read this firstExperts say buying property abroad falls under capital account transactions, which come with strict rules. Credit cards are meant for daily expenses like travel or shopping, not for large investments like real estate.
Sensex ends 1,200 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; Titan up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1205.00 points to end at 75,273.45, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 394.05 points to close at 23,306.45.

Can mutual funds be gifted? All you need to know about Sebi's PPI planThe idea is simple. A person can purchase a Gift PPI using bank transfer or UPI. This prepaid card can then be gifted to another person. The recipient can redeem the card on an asset management company's platform and invest the amount in mutual funds.

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver rises Rs 14,000: Smart buying opportunity or risk?Gold and silver have made a strong comeback, drawing fresh attention from investors. But with prices rising so quickly, is this a good time to buy or a moment to pause and reassess?

41% borrowers are Gen Z: Is hidden debt rising among young Indians?A growing number of young Indians are turning to credit early in life. With Gen Z now making up 41% of borrowers, is this a sign of financial confidence or a warning of rising hidden debt? Let's find out.

LPG booking timelines unchanged; Centre assures steady supplyAuthorities have urged people not to believe or share unverified information. They have also advised against panic booking of cylinders, stressing that there is enough supply available across the country.

8th Pay Commission: Can government salaries finally match private sector perks?For years, government employees have measured their pay against private sector packages. Now, with the 8th Pay Commission seeking feedback, will it lead to a more balanced pay structure? Let's have a look.

entertainment.headline: Godfather's Godfather: RGV wants to wipe out his past after Dhurandhar 2 Ram Gopal Varma says he wants to "wipe out" his past after watching Dhurandhar: The Revenge, calling it a game-changer. The filmmaker also explains why he chose The Syndicate over Sarkar 4, and what impresses him the most about director Aditya Dhar.

Ek Din's new song Khwaab Dekhoon deepens Junaid and Sai's romantic tale A new song from Ek Din offers a deeper look into Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi's romance. Here's why Khwaab Dekhoon is drawing attention.

Meet Reble: The Northeast rapper who brought fresh edge to Dhurandhar soundtrack  Meghalaya-born rapper Reble is making waves with her powerhouse performances on Dhurandhar's soundtrack. Discover how she's redefining Indian hip-hop.

Kannada superstar Shiva Rajkumar reviews Dhurandhar 2, highlights vintage music Kannada superstar Shiva Rajkumar wrote a note appreciating Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He also highlighted Ranveer Singh's performance and the choice of music in the film.

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's Jaskirat just redefined Bollywood's defeatist hero From Guru Dutt's tragic loners in Pyaasa and Kaagaz ke Phool to Amitabh Bachchan's emotionally burdened rebels, Hindi cinema has seen defeatist heroes before. But Ranveer Singh's Jaskirat Singh Rangi in Dhurandhar: The Revenge reframes loss, vulnerability and endurance as markers of contemporary cool.

Netflix, Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Mardaani 3, O'Romeo, Kaattaan and more This week's streaming lineup on Netflix, Prime Video, and JioHotstar offers something for everyone, from action-packed thrillers like Mardaani 3 and O'Romeo to emotional dramas and music documentaries like Masthishka Maranam and BTS: The Return.

Mardaani 3 on OTT: When and where to watch Rani Mukerji's cop-drama Rani Mukerji's Mardaani 3, the gripping cop thriller, is all set to premiere on Netflix. The film, praised for its strong storyline and powerful performances, has already made a mark at the box office with impressive earnings.

Dhurandhar 2 is the picture of new India: Kangana Ranaut at IFFD 2026 Kangana Ranaut called Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge the picture of a "new India" at International Film Festival of Delhi (IFFD) 2026. She highlighted the need to encourage such films on platforms as IFFD.

Ryan Gosling's Project Hail Mary IMAX edged out in India? Here's the truth Designed for IMAX, Project Hail Mary initially saw limited shows in India as Dhurandhar 2 held screens. However, additional shows were added following the online backlash.
Malayalam actor-director EA Rajendran dies at 71 after prolonged illness Veteran Malayalam actor-director EA Rajendran died on Thursday after a prolonged illness. He was 71.

Dhurandhar action director Aejaz Gulab defends film's violence: Not mindless action Action director Aejaz Gulab emphasises the emotional realism behind Dhurandhar's intense action scenes, saying the film does not feature "mindless action."

Yami Gautam's Dhurandhar 2 cameo: A smart nod to Uri and Haq Yami Gautam's short cameo in Dhurandhar: The Revenge has grabbed viewers' attention because it reminds them of her earlier roles. From the name "Shazia Bano" to the undercover nurse angle, the scene brings back memories of Haq and Uri: The Surgical Strike.

Ram Charan's eyelid injury a moment of concern, Chiranjeevi thanks doctor Chiranjeevi publicly appreciated the Hyderabad-based doctor for performing surgery on Ram Charan's eyelid. The RRR actor suffered an injury to his eye during the shooting for Peddi.

Ustaad Bhagat Singh director reacts to trolls amid film's negative reviews Director Harish Shankar broke his silence on the poor reviews for his recently released Pawan Kalyan film, Ustaad Bhagat Singh. He said that he'd listen to constructive criticism and how online trolling doesn't affect him anymore.

Vivek Oberoi praises Aditya Dhar, says Dhurandhar 2 projects India globally Vivek Oberoi praised Aditya Dhar and the team behind Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He highlighted how the film projects India's voice on a global stage.

Jr NTR hosts fun-filled birthday bash for wife Pranathi in Hyderabad. See pics For his wife Pranathi's birthday, actor Jr NTR threw a bash to celebrate her speciald ay. Celebrities, including SS Karthikeya and Swapna Dutt attended the birthday bash.

Actor Mustafa Ahmed on Dhurandhar 2 propaganda claims: I respect everyone's agenda Mustafa Ahmed has addressed claims that Dhurandhar: The Revenge promotes propaganda. The actor dismissed the idea, saying the film's intent is not driven by any agenda.

Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone teaser out, series set for Christmas 2026 The much-awaited Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone series teaser has been released, offering fans a glimpse into Harry's early life and magical journey. The show is set to premiere during Christmas 2026.

AI-generated, fake: Aditya Dhar warns as Ranveer's smoking-in-turban pic goes viral Aditya Dhar breaks his silence on a viral image of Ranveer Singh's Hamza smoking in a turban, calling it fake and AI-manipulated. The Dhurandhar: The Revenge director issues a stern warning and addresses concerns around the portrayal.

Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) have tied up imports in advance, ensuring there is no gap in supply.

Air India London flight makes U-turn over Saudi, lands in Delhi after 7 hoursFlightradar data showed that the Air India A350 aircraft (AI 111), en route to London's Heathrow airport from Delhi, made a turnaround while it was flying over Saudi Arabian airspace.

Chief of Iran's elite naval force, responsible for Hormuz blockade, killed: IsraelIf confirmed, the killing of IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri would deal a major blow to Iran's military leadership, particularly its naval operations in the strategically vital Persian Gulf.

PM to speak to Chief Ministers tomorrow to review preparedness amid Iran warThe meeting will focus on preparedness measures, including supply chains, energy security, and the safety of Indian citizens abroad.

Tanker loaded with Russian oil struck by drone boat in Black Sea, says TurkeyThe broadcaster said there was an explosion on the bridge of the ship, owned by Turkish firm Besiktas. It added that the explosion caused water to enter the oil tanker's engine room.

US is fighting Iran. A Nato ally is preparing to fight USAs the US wages war on Iran, fractures are emerging not just within Republican ranks but across America's global alliances. Nato allies are keeping their distance. Denmark even prepared for a war over Trump's Greenland push. Trump's choices are seemingly leaving Washington increasingly isolated.

Video: Professor proposes to student inside classroom, she slaps him with slipperA professor at a medical college near Bengaluru was allegedly thrashed by students after he publicly proposed to a student during class, leading to a heated confrontation.

Trump claims Iran proposed making him Supreme Leader: I said, 'No, thank you'US President Donald Trump made the extraordinary claim that Iran's leadership had informally floated the idea of him becoming the Islamic Republic's next Supreme Leader, but he turned them down.

Hormuz opens for India: Iran says 5 'friendly nations' won't face blockadeIn an interview with Iranian state TV, Abbas Araghchi said that the Strait of Hormuz is not entirely closed, as several friendly nations are allowed to pass through.

Live: Pakistan foreign minister says indirect talks between US, Iran underwayUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: In the fourth week of the conflict, Washington has sent a 15-point truce proposal to Tehran aimed at ending hostilities in the Middle East. The US is also pushing for a month-long ceasefire to facilitate negotiations. Iran says it is reviewing the proposal but maintains that no formal talks with Washington are underway. With Iran's military dismissing former US President Donald Trump's claims of ongoing negotiations, the likelihood of Tehran accepting any deal that compromises its nuclear programme appears slim. Meanwhile, hostilities continue unabated, with Israel launching fresh airstrikes targeting multiple locations in Tehran on Wednesday. In response, Iran has carried out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military bases in the Gulf region. Follow all the latest updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict on IndiaToday.in.

Vande Bharat passenger finds worms in food, IRCTC fined Rs 10 lakh after complaintThe incident came to light after Ritesh Kumar Singh recorded a video shortly after being served his meal on the Patna–Tatanagar Vande Bharat Express on March 15.

More ODIs for Virat and Rohit as BCCI announces 2026-27 home season scheduleThe BCCI announced the schedule for the senior men's team's 2026-27 home season on Thursday, 26 March. A total of nine ODIs will be played in this period, offering fans ample opportunities to watch Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma in action.

16 US aircraft lost in Iran war, radar defences ripped openAs the US-Israel-Iran war nears its first month, American air losses are rising. Over the course of the conflict, the US has lost at least 16 aircraft, with several more damaged, while key radar sites have also been hit. Here's what we know.

Viral video of 'stolen' dogs on highway in China has a twist you didn't expectThe viral video, which was circulated across several social media platforms, showed a group of dogs walking together along a busy highway in Changchun in Jilin province.

Godfather's Godfather: RGV wants to wipe out his past after Dhurandhar 2Ram Gopal Varma says he wants to "wipe out" his past after watching Dhurandhar: The Revenge, calling it a game-changer. The filmmaker also explains why he chose The Syndicate over Sarkar 4, and what impresses him the most about director Aditya Dhar.

Russia dismisses report it is sending drones to Iran amid Middle East warThe Financial Times had reported that discussions between high-ranking Iranian and Russian officials about the delivery of drones began days after Israel and the US initiated their joint military campaign against Tehran. The claim was attributed to two unnamed officials briefed on the intelligence.

River Indie review, first ride: Is this really an SUV in scooter clothingThe River Indie stands out as a distinctive offering in the electric scooter market. While most manufacturers highlight performance as their primary USP, River positions its scooter as the SUV of the segment, a claim that immediately sparks curiosity.

LSG owner credits Jay Shah for IPL boom, Lalit Modi tells him to jog his memoryIPL 2026: Indian Premier League founder Lalit Modi has hit out at LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka for crediting the league's success to former BCCI boss Jay Shah. In a scathing tweet, Modi called out Goenka and told him to jog his memory.

How blood diamonds became secret war currency, and trapped children in minesBlood diamonds are more than rare gemstones. Mined in conflict zones and sold through hidden networks, they have funded wars, armed militias and relied on child labour. As global tensions rise again, here's how diamonds became part of the machinery of conflict, and why the debate keeps returning during wars.

Dhurandhar 2 worldwide box office live: Aamir Khan on success of Aditya Dhar's filmDhurandhar 2 Box Office Collection Live Updates: The Aditya Dhar directorial continues its revenge at the ticket window by smashing left, right, centre. The film has comfortably entered the Rs 1,000-crore club in seven days. The buzz has now entered Delhi assembly as well.
Saturn's rings seen glowing in new Webb Telescope pictures. See picsIn these latest images, the planet's rings appear as bright, icy white structures, almost glowing against the darkness of space.

Why India is pushing LPG users to switch to PNG | Explained in 5 pointsEnergy disruption due to the US-Iran war has exposed India's dependence on imported gas. It has prompted a policy shift mandating a switch from LPG to piped natural gas (PNG) where available. With supply constraints looming, the government is fast-tracking pipelines and pushing a nationwide transition to strengthen energy security.

sports.headline: Rodri denies feud with Vinicius Jr, open to La Liga transfer Manchester City midfielder Rodri denies any rift with Vinícius Junior after the Ballon d'Or controversy and hints at a possible move to Madrid, potentially in La Liga, as his Manchester City contract nears expiry.

Chennai, Ahmedabad among venues to host Border-Gavaskar Trophy 2027 India will host Australia in the much-anticipated Border-Gavaskar Trophy, with the five-Test series beginning on January 21, 2027, in Nagpur.

More ODIs for Virat and Rohit as BCCI announces 2026-27 home season schedule The BCCI announced the schedule for the senior men's team's 2026-27 home season on Thursday, 26 March. A total of nine ODIs will be played in this period, offering fans ample opportunities to watch Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma in action.

Senegal approaches CAS after controversially being stripped of AFCON title Senegal have approached the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) after being stripped of their Africa Cup of Nations title by CAF. The governing body controversially awarded the crown to Morocco following a chaotic final. The appeal could take months, with no clear timeline yet set for a verdict.

IPL 2026: How Rishabh Pant trained under Yuvraj Singh in pursuit of LSG redemption Former India cricketer Irfan Pathan has shed light on the intense preparation Rishabh Pant is undergoing with veteran Yuvraj Singh ahead of the crucial Indian Premier League 2026 season.

Kylian Mbappe denies that Real Madrid examined the wrong knee after injury Kylian Mbappe has denied reports that his knee injury was misdiagnosed after Real Madrid's medical staff allegedly examined the wrong knee while attempting to treat the issue.

LSG owner credits Jay Shah for IPL boom, Lalit Modi tells him to jog his memory IPL 2026: Indian Premier League founder Lalit Modi has hit out at LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka for crediting the league's success to former BCCI boss Jay Shah. In a scathing tweet, Modi called out Goenka and told him to jog his memory.

IPL 2026: Pat Cummins targets mid-season return for SRH after back injury IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins has said that his best-case scenario is to return to full fitness for the second half of the IPL, including the knockout stages in late May.

Gujarat Titans Preview: About time Shubman Gill steps up as captain IPL 2026: Shubman Gill has been exceptional with the bat for Gujarat Titans, but he is still searching for a defining impact as captain. This season, the big question is whether he can lead the Titans to a second IPL title - their first since Hardik Pandya guided them to glory in 2022.

Rinku Singh appointed Regional Sports Officer, thanks UP government for honour Indian cricketer Rinku Singh on Wednesday thanked the Uttar Pradesh Government and Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath for appointing him as a Regional Sports Officer under the sports quota.

Meet Mangesh Yadav: Son of a truck driver, now set to live IPL dream at RCB IPL 2026: Madhya Pradesh fast bowler Mangesh Yadav is set for his maiden stint with the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, who bought the pacer for Rs 5.20 crore in the mini auction last year.

Madness paid off: Vijay Mallya on RCB's Rs 450 crore to Rs 16,500 crore journeyIPL 2026: Former Royal Challengers Bengaluru owner Vijay Mallya took a victory lap on X after the team was sold for Rs 16,500 crore. Mallya revealed that he had originally bought the franchise for Rs 450 crore, an investment that has multiplied to 37 times its original value.

CSK set to have new opening combination for IPL 2026: Captain Ruturaj GaikwadIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have revealed their opening combination for the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League. CSK captain Ruturaj Gaikwad revealed that the team will be shaking up their batting order this season.

Pakistan Super League 2026: Squads, schedule and all you need to knowPSL 2026 begins on March 26 with defending champions Lahore Qalandars facing debutants Hyderabad Kingsmen at Gaddafi Stadium, as the expanded eight-team tournament runs until May 3 with 44 matches.

Loved young MS Dhoni more, he is India's greatest white-ball captain: Sourav GangulyFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly said he preferred the younger, fearless version of MS Dhoni, recalling how the wicketkeeper-batter from Jharkhand could effortlessly hit sixes under pressure. Dhoni made his international debut under Ganguly's captaincy in 2004.

Who doesn't want to play for India? Prithvi Shaw eyes strong season for DCIPL 2026: Prithvi Shaw eyes India comeback after move to Maharashtra and return to Delhi Capitals, aiming to rediscover form and consistency in IPL 2026 after a difficult phase in his career.

Impact Player to ball change: What was discussed at IPL captains' meet?BCCI officials and franchise captains met in Mumbai to finalise IPL 2026 playing conditions, addressing the contentious Impact Player rule, ball-change protocols for dew, and stricter match-day training guidelines.

Navdeep Saini replaces injured Harshit Rana for Kolkata Knight RidersIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders have roped in Navdeep Saini as a replacement for Harshi Rana, who has been ruled out of the tournament due to a igament strain in his right knee.

Will RCB bowling struggle without Hazlewood, Yash Dayal? Aakash Chopra reactsAakash Chopra recently shared his views on RCB's bowling lineup, expressing concerns that the team could face challenges with Josh Hazlewood set to miss a few matches and Yash Dayal unavailable for the entire season.

From a challenge to a legacy: How Naveen John's unlikely cycling journey startedA challenge with his college friend made Naveen John start a side quest to come back to India to become a national champion in cycling. The 39-year-old opens up on his journey in the sport, not chasing the Olympic dream as an athlete but setting it up for future talent.

Arshdeep Singh struggling in T20s? Irfan Pathan analyses India pacer's Achilles heelFormer India all-rounder Irfan Pathan has urged Arshdeep Singh to improve his new-ball bowling ahead of IPL 2026, pointing to a lack of inswing and a dip in effectiveness since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Ashwin's talking bull****: Yograj slams India legend for writing off Arjun TendulkarIPL 2026: Yograj Singh fires back at Ashwin for writing off Arjun Tendulkar at LSG, saying he could make him surpass every batsman in the world within six months.

IPL 2026 preview: Injuries pile up, but do KKR have a Rahane problem too?IPL 2026: Three-time champions Kolkata Knight Riders will be aiming for a successful campaign after an underwhelming outing in 2025. But with injuries mounting and a slight combination issue, can Kolkata spread some joy to their fans?

Villain Gautam Gambhir doesn't worry about being liked by anyone: Ex-CSK starIPL 2026: Former CSK star Faf du Plessis and ex-India opener Aakash Chopra praised Gautam Gambhir's leadership and weighed in on whether Kolkata Knight Riders can thrive without their former mentor.

No stupid videos: Jason Gillespie makes his intent clear on Pakistan return in PSLJason Gillespie's return to Pakistan cricket ahead of PSL 2026 has created a buzz, with the former Pakistan red-ball coach going viral for bluntly refusing to film a "stupid" arrival video for Hyderabad Kingsmen.

Incredible Amelia Kerr joins Rohit Sharma in elite club with 2nd T20I centuryNZW vs SAW: Amelia Kerr continued her incredible run as captain after scoring a 52-ball century in the fifth and final T20I against South Africa at the Hagley Oval in Christchurch.

Injury or routine visit? Why Jasprit Bumrah is at BCCI COE before IPLIPL 2026: Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah has not been part of the Mumbai Indians training camp. He checked into the BCCI Centre for Excellence in Bengaluru, but speculation about a fresh injury has now been debunked.

Gautam Gambhir gets personality rights protection in deepfake caseThe Delhi High Court has granted Gautam Gambhir protection of his personality rights and ordered the removal of objectionable deepfake and misleading content circulating across social media and internet platforms.

5th T20I: Esterhuizen, bowlers power South Africa to 3–2 series win vs New ZealandSouth Africa secured their first away T20I series under Shukri Conrad and a second successive bilateral series with a convincing performance against New Zealand in Christchurch.

Ben Duckett's withdrawal raises concerns over English players' future in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Ben Duckett's withdrawal from the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League has sparked fresh concerns over the reliability of English players in the league, with former India opener Aakash Chopra warning that franchises may rethink investing in young cricketers from England.

Dark chocolate for dark boy: L Sivaramakrishnan says he faced racism from teammatesLaxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed how teenage encounters with racism and systemic bias shattered his confidence, offering a haunting new perspective on why one of India's greatest bowling prodigies saw his career unravel.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
